name: Inverness Clubhouse
desc: 2072 Azalea Dr — the heart of the neighborhood.
detail: The gathering room and point of reference for everything else.
cardDesc: 2072 Azalea Drive, Roswell, GA 30075. The neighborhood's gathering room and the point of reference for everything else.

name: Chattahoochee Nature Center
desc: 127 acres of trails, wildlife rehab, and river access.
detail: A unit of the Chattahoochee River National Recreation Area.
cardDesc: 127 acres of forest, meadow, and rehabilitated wildlife along the river — a unit of the Chattahoochee River National Recreation Area.

name: Roswell Riverwalk Boardwalk and Trail
desc: A scenic boardwalk and 10+ miles of paved multi-use trail along the Chattahoochee.
detail: Begins at the dam at Morgan Falls Park, follows the river, and extends eastward to connect with the Vickery Creek Trail and beyond.
cardDesc: A scenic boardwalk and paved riverside trail beginning at Morgan Falls Park dam, winding alongside the Chattahoochee, and extending 3+ miles eastward past the Vickery Creek Trail all the way to East Roswell — perfect for walking, running, and cycling.

name: Roswell Town Square
desc: The 1839 square, with the original bandstand.
detail: Mill workers' cottages and Barrington Hall across the street.
cardDesc: Laid out in 1839 by Roswell King, with the original bandstand, the founding cottages, and Barrington Hall across the street.

name: Downtown Roswell
desc: Restaurants, shops, and galleries along Canton Street.
detail: The monthly Alive After Five street festival, April through October.
cardDesc: Canton Street's restaurants, galleries, and the monthly Alive After Five street festival, April through October.

name: Azalea Park
desc: A wooded playground and picnic area directly on the Chattahoochee River.
detail: Shaded picnic tables, natural playground, and riverfront trails for a peaceful escape.
cardDesc: A shaded, riverfront playground and picnic area with natural wooded trails along the Chattahoochee — a peaceful spot for families and nature lovers.

name: Azalea Boat Launch
desc: River access for kayaks, canoes, and fishing along the Chattahoochee.
detail: A popular put-in and take-out spot for paddlers exploring the National Recreation Area.
cardDesc: A concrete boat launch and parking area for kayaks, canoes, and fishing — a popular access point for paddling the Chattahoochee River National Recreation Area.

name: Riverside Park
desc: A sprawling park with playgrounds, spraygrounds, and an amphitheater.
detail: Hosts summer concerts, movie nights, and community festivals for the whole family.
cardDesc: A sprawling riverfront park with a massive playground, spraygrounds, and an outdoor amphitheater — home to summer concerts, movie nights, and community festivals.

name: Riverside Recreation Area
desc: Outdoor volleyball courts and fitness trails along the river.
detail: Open-air courts and exercise stations with scenic Chattahoochee River views.
cardDesc: Open-air volleyball courts and outdoor fitness stations set along the riverbank — a scenic spot for active recreation with Chattahoochee River views.
